pr.title: Identify remaining functions in lbl tables
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Identified and typed several remaining function-pointer tables that had been represented as generic lbl data, reducing the remaining unknown-function tables to 17 un_* tables according to the PR body. The changes added concrete table declarations in gm, lb, and audio code, corrected symbol sizes/data widths, and propagated function signatures from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or int/s32 to bool/void typed callbacks. Review discussion focused on matching a ColorOverlay byte/word access in lb_00F9 and suggested using a better local match plus a union for the light_color representation.
what_changed: Split and corrected symbol-table entries for previously broad lbl objects, including changing lbl_803BCA24 from one 0xF4 object into lbl_803BCA24, lbl_803BCA4C, and lbl_803BCAC8 with separate sizes.
Changed lbl_803D6300 metadata in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from data:2byte to data:4byte, matching its use as an array of u32/function-pointer pairs.

Added struct lbl_803D6300_t in gm_16F1.c with fields u32 x0 and bool (*x4)(void), then initialized lbl_803D6300 with many known callbacks such as fn_801735F0, gm_80164ABC, fn_80162CCC, gmMainLib_8015D508, and gmMainLib_8015CF94.
Updated gm_1601.h and gm_16F1.h prototypes so table callbacks return bool and take no parameters where appropriate instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or int.

Added typedef lbl_803D9FD8_fn as void (*)(s32*, u32, u32) in gm_18A5.c and initialized lbl_803D9FD8 with menu-related callbacks such as fn_801937C4, fn_80193B58, fn_80194D84, fn_80195AF0, and fn_8019610C.
Updated gm_18A5.h to give the lbl_803D9FD8 callback functions concrete void return and (s32*, u32, u32) parameter signatures.
Changed gmMainLib_8015CF94 and gmMainLib_8015D508 from s32-returning functions to bool-returning functions in both source and header to fit their callback-table usage.

Implemented several small gmresultplayer callback wrappers and camera draw callbacks: fn_80179D3C through fn_80179DA8 forward to fn_80179990 with constant mode indices, while fn_80179DCC through fn_80179F04 set the current CObj, call Camera_800313E0, set gxlink_prios to 0x80, call HSD_GObj_80390ED0, then end the current CObj.

Added lb_803BA248 as a bool (*)(ColorOverlay*) function-pointer table in lb_00F9.c and retitled several ColorOverlay command handlers from int or ad-hoc struct arguments to bool ColorOverlay* callbacks.
Added lbl_803BCA24 as a bool (*)(HSD_GObj*) table in lbaudio_ax.c, identifying audio callback entries fn_800251EC through fn_80025E38.
Adjusted related headers and static headers so declarations match the newly identified callback signatures.

Modified tools/table-typer/main.go and tools/table-typer/c.go, though the provided excerpt does not show enough detail to summarize the exact tooling algorithm changes beyond their relationship to table typing.
smart_moves: Used the structure of data tables to infer function prototypes and then propagated those prototypes consistently through source and headers.

Represented lbl_803D6300 as a small explicit struct containing a u32 key/flags field and a bool callback pointer, making the 4-byte data interpretation visible in C rather than leaving it as anonymous lbl data.
Introduced local typedefs for callback arrays such as lb_803BA248_fn, lbl_803BCA24_fn, and lbl_803D9FD8_fn, which keeps table declarations readable and forces consistent signatures across entries.
Matched tiny wrapper functions in gmresultplayer by recognizing repeated forwarding patterns and repeated camera render callback bodies.
Converted return types from int/s32 to bool where table semantics showed the functions were predicates or boolean callbacks.
Split oversized symbol entries where one lbl object actually contained multiple adjacent tables, improving future table discovery and symbol search precision.
decomp_lessons: Function-pointer tables are strong evidence for callback signatures: once one table type is known, every referenced function should be checked and retitled to the same return and parameter shape if matching supports it.
Symbol metadata can block or obscure table identification; correcting object boundaries and data width in symbols.txt is part of the decomp fix, not just cleanup.
Prefer named callback typedefs for lbl tables because they make wrong prototypes easier to spot in headers and cross-file references.
When a data table alternates scalar words and pointers, model it as a struct array rather than parallel anonymous data if the layout supports it.
Repeated small wrapper functions often encode table-specific constant arguments; matching them can unlock callback tables without needing to fully understand the callee.

For color or packed data accesses, matching may depend on forcing a word load instead of byte loads; a union or packed representation can be cleaner than pointer-casting, according to review discussion.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based lbl names for unidentified data tables, e.g. lbl_803D6300, lbl_803D9FD8, lbl_803BCA24, and lb_803BA248.
Used table-specific typedef names ending in _fn, such as lbl_803D9FD8_fn, lbl_803BCA24_fn, and lb_803BA248_fn.
Used anonymous field names with offsets for newly modeled table structs where semantics are still weak, e.g. struct lbl_803D6300_t with x0 and x4.
Preserved existing address-based function names such as fn_801722BC and subsystem-prefixed names such as gmMainLib_8015D508 when only signatures, not semantic names, were identified.
ColorOverlay fields follow offset-style descriptive names such as x0_timer, x8_ptr1, x50_light_color, x7C_color_enable, and x7C_flag2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed lbl_803D6300 from data:2byte to data:4byte after identifying it as entries containing 32-bit values plus function pointers.
Used explicit callback return types such as bool and void to match table consumers and eliminate generic UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations.

For lb_80013BB8, the submitted code used a cast through void to read light_color as s32 and mask it with 0x03FFFFFF, because accessing individual members reportedly compiled to byte loads instead of the desired word load.
Review feedback suggested a union-based representation for light_color to obtain a better match than raw pointer casting.

Repeated camera callback bodies in gmresultplayer use HSD_CObjSetCurrent, Camera_800313E0, gxlink_prios assignment, HSD_GObj_80390ED0, and HSD_CObjEndCurrent, indicating a reusable matching pattern for render callbacks.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Noted uncertainty about the ColorOverlay light_color access and said member access compiled to lbz instead of the desired lbw.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Said not to worry because a local match existed, then linked to matching versions of lb_00F9.c.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Pointed to a better local version and specifically mentioned using a union, with a link to the relevant types.h lines.

searchable_terms: Identify remaining functions in lbl tables
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that update lb_00F9 ColorOverlay light_color to use a union instead of pointer-casting.
Search for remaining un_803F9FA4 through un_803FDAF8 tables mentioned as the final 17 unknown-function tables.
Inspect tools/table-typer changes around PR 1846 to learn how the table identification workflow was improved.
Search for later semantic renames of lbl_803D6300, lbl_803D9FD8, lbl_803BCA24, and lb_803BA248.
Find follow-up PRs that further type gmresultplayer callbacks fn_80179D3C through fn_80179F04.
